findings.title: No Machine Identity Authentication
findings.description: API Gateway endpoints have no authorization configured. All three REST APIs (Product, Basket, Order) are publicly accessible without authentication. No OAuth2, API keys, mTLS, Cognito authorizers, or IAM authorization.
findings.gap: No machine identity mechanism exists. Cannot attribute API calls to any principal or distinguish callers.
findings.recommendation: Implement API Gateway authorization (IAM SigV4, Cognito, or API keys with usage plans) on all methods in lib/apigateway.ts.

findings.title: No Machine Identity Authentication
findings.description: The application uses Amazon Cognito for authentication on POST /books with implicit OAuth flow and USER_PASSWORD_AUTH only. GET /books requires no authentication. No client_credentials flow, API keys, or mTLS configured for machine-to-machine authentication.
findings.gap: No machine identity authentication mechanism exists. Cognito is configured for human user authentication only.
findings.recommendation: Add a Cognito App Client with client_credentials grant type and configure a resource server with custom scopes (e.g., books/read, books/write). Alternatively, enable API Gateway API keys with a usage plan.

findings.title: No Machine Identity Authentication
findings.description: The application uses session-based authentication only (PHP session_start() with cookie-based session tracking). No support for machine identity — no OAuth2 client credentials, no API key authentication with principal attribution, no mTLS.
findings.gap: No machine identity authentication mechanism exists. Agents cannot authenticate without browser-like session management.
findings.recommendation: Implement API key authentication with principal attribution alongside existing session auth. Add Authorization: Bearer <api-key> header check mapping keys to named service accounts.

findings.title: No Machine Identity Authentication
findings.description: OAuth2 configured with @EnableResourceServer but all endpoints set to permitAll(). No authentication enforced. No machine identity (service account, API key, mTLS, or client credentials flow) exists.
findings.gap: No mechanism to authenticate agent callers or attribute actions to specific agent identities.
findings.recommendation: Implement API Gateway with IAM/Cognito authorizers or OAuth2 client credentials flow with principal attribution.

findings.title: No Machine-Readable API Specification
findings.description: No OpenAPI, Swagger, AsyncAPI, GraphQL schema, or Smithy specification files exist. API structure defined only in CDK constructs and Lambda code.
findings.gap: No machine-readable spec for auto-generating agent tool definitions.
findings.recommendation: Create OpenAPI specification documenting all endpoints, request/response schemas, and error codes.

findings.title: Unstructured Error Responses
findings.description: Errors return HTTP 500 with { message, errorMsg, errorStack }. No error codes, no retryable indicators. All errors conflated as 500. Stack traces exposed.
findings.gap: Agents cannot distinguish retriable from terminal errors. Stack trace exposure is a security concern.
findings.recommendation: Add error codes and retryable boolean. Differentiate 400/500. Remove stack traces.

findings.title: No Scoped Permissions for Agent Identity
findings.description: All Lambda functions receive grantReadWriteData to their DynamoDB tables. No mechanism exists to create a scoped agent identity with read-only access.
findings.gap: Cannot create a read-only agent identity. All callers get the same broad access level.
findings.recommendation: Implement per-method IAM authorization that differentiates read vs write access.

findings.title: No Action-Level Authorization
findings.description: No action-level authorization exists. No auth middleware, no per-method authorization, no RBAC or ABAC. Any caller can invoke any HTTP method on any endpoint.
findings.gap: Cannot enforce read-only access for agents within a resource type.
findings.recommendation: Implement IAM authorization with per-method policy conditions on execute-api:method.

findings.title: No Identity Propagation or Delegation
findings.description: No identity propagation mechanism exists. No JWT parsing, token exchange, on-behalf-of flows, or user context headers. Cannot distinguish agent-as-self from agent-on-behalf-of-user.
findings.gap: Cannot determine whose data an agent accesses. Ordering service returns data by userName without verifying caller authority.
findings.recommendation: Design on-behalf-of flow with AUTH-Q1 implementation. Propagate caller identity via API Gateway context.

findings.title: No Agent Identity Suspension Capability
findings.description: No authentication mechanism exists, therefore no mechanism to suspend or revoke individual agent identities. Cannot isolate a misbehaving agent without shutting down the entire API.
findings.gap: No per-agent revocation capability.
findings.recommendation: Implement API key authentication with usage plans providing per-key revocation.

findings.title: No Circuit Breakers or Resilience Patterns
findings.description: Basket service calls EventBridge and DynamoDB without circuit breakers, explicit retry, or timeout config. SQS queue has no DLQ. No resilience patterns exist.
findings.gap: Cascading failures propagate uncontrolled. Failed SQS messages retry indefinitely without DLQ.
findings.recommendation: Add DLQ to SQS queue. Configure Lambda timeouts. Consider AWS Lambda Powertools.

findings.title: No Rate Limiting or Throttling
findings.description: No rate limiting configured on API Gateway. No usage plans, WAF, or per-client throttling. Only AWS account-level limits provide protection.
findings.gap: Runaway agent loops can overwhelm services at machine speed.
findings.recommendation: Add deployOptions with throttle settings. Create usage plans with per-key rate limits.

findings.title: No Graceful Degradation Signaling
findings.description: No degradation signaling. No health endpoints, no X-Degraded headers, no Cache-Control headers. Generic 500 on all failures.
findings.gap: Agents cannot detect when receiving degraded vs authoritative responses.
findings.recommendation: Add health endpoint with granular status. Include degradation headers.

findings.title: Sensitive Data Exposed in Agent-Facing API Responses
findings.description: Order table stores PII (firstName, lastName, email, address, paymentMethod, cardInfo). GET /order endpoints return all fields including PII without filtering.
findings.gap: Agent-facing APIs return sensitive PII fields without field-level filtering.
findings.recommendation: Implement field projection in ordering Lambda to exclude PII from GET responses intended for agent consumption.

findings.title: No Data Residency Controls for PII
findings.description: Order table stores customer PII in DynamoDB without explicit region pinning, data residency documentation, or cross-region replication restrictions.
findings.gap: No documented data residency controls. Agent could transmit PII to LLM provider in different jurisdiction.
findings.recommendation: Document data residency requirements. Pin CDK stack to specific region. Filter PII from agent access.

findings.title: PII Logged to CloudWatch Without Redaction
findings.description: All Lambda handlers log full event payloads including PII (names, email, addresses, payment card info) to CloudWatch Logs without any redaction or masking.
findings.gap: Customer PII written to logs without redaction. Compliance violation risk.
findings.recommendation: Remove full event logging. Implement structured logging excluding PII. Apply CloudWatch Logs data protection policies.

findings.title: No Pagination or Selective Query Support
findings.description: GET /product and GET /order perform full DynamoDB table scans without pagination, filtering, or result size limits.
findings.gap: Agents get unbounded result sets that can exhaust LLM context windows.
findings.recommendation: Add Limit and ExclusiveStartKey parameters for cursor-based pagination.

findings.title: No Input Validation or Schema Enforcement
findings.description: No input validation in any Lambda handler. POST/PUT endpoints parse JSON and write directly to DynamoDB without field validation or type checking.
findings.gap: Malformed agent payloads persisted without rejection. No structured validation errors.
findings.recommendation: Add API Gateway request validators with JSON schemas for POST/PUT endpoints.

findings.title: Incomplete Temporal Metadata
findings.description: Orders have orderDate. Products and baskets lack created_at/updated_at fields. No Cache-Control or freshness headers.
findings.gap: Cannot determine data freshness for products or baskets.
findings.recommendation: Add createdAt and updatedAt fields. Include Last-Modified headers.

findings.title: No Schema Versioning or API Contracts
findings.description: No API versioning, no schema documentation, no breaking change detection in CI, no consumer-driven contract tests.
findings.gap: API changes break agent tool bindings silently.
findings.recommendation: Add /v1/ prefix to routes. Implement OpenAPI spec validation in CI.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing or Structured Logging
findings.description: No X-Ray or OpenTelemetry tracing. Unstructured console.log without correlation IDs or trace propagation.
findings.gap: Cannot trace agent-initiated requests end-to-end.
findings.recommendation: Enable X-Ray active tracing. Add structured JSON logging with correlation IDs.

findings.title: No Alerting on Error Rates or Latency
findings.description: No CloudWatch alarms, anomaly detection, or alerting configuration exists.
findings.gap: System degradation affecting agent consumers goes undetected.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch alarms for Lambda error rate, API Gateway 5xx rate, and P99 latency.

findings.title: IaC Present but No Governance Controls
findings.description: CDK IaC exists but no CI/CD enforces peer review and no drift detection is configured.
findings.gap: Changes unreviewed; drift undetected.
findings.recommendation: Add CI pipeline with cdk diff on PRs. Enable branch protection. Add Config drift detection.

findings.title: No CI/CD or Contract Testing
findings.description: No CI/CD pipeline. Only test file is entirely commented out. No automated testing on changes.
findings.gap: API-breaking changes cannot be caught before production.
findings.recommendation: Create CI pipeline: cdk synth + API integration tests + OpenAPI validation.

findings.title: No Controlled Rollback Capability
findings.description: No blue/green, canary, CodeDeploy, or feature flags. Only CloudFormation default rollback on failed deployments.
findings.gap: Cannot quickly roll back behaviorally broken deployments.
findings.recommendation: Implement Lambda aliases + CodeDeploy with automatic rollback on alarm triggers.

findings.title: Zero Effective Test Coverage
findings.description: Single test file is entirely commented out. No API tests, integration tests, or contract tests.
findings.gap: No automated verification of API behavior.
findings.recommendation: Write integration tests for all API endpoints.

findings.title: No Customer-Managed Encryption for PII Data
findings.description: DynamoDB default encryption (AWS-owned keys). No customer-managed KMS keys for order table containing PII.
findings.gap: Data encrypted with AWS-owned keys rather than CMK for PII.
findings.recommendation: Add TableEncryption.CUSTOMER_MANAGED with KMS key to order table.

findings.title: No Machine-Readable API Specification
findings.description: No OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, GraphQL schema, or Smithy specification file exists. The API surface is defined only in the SAM template and source code.
findings.gap: No machine-readable API specification for automated agent tool generation.
findings.recommendation: Generate an OpenAPI 3.0 spec. SAM supports inline OpenAPI via the DefinitionBody property.

findings.title: No Structured Error Responses
findings.description: Error responses return HTTP 500 with empty body and empty headers. No error code, message, or retryable indicator.
findings.gap: Empty error response body provides zero diagnostic information for agents.
findings.recommendation: Implement standard error envelope: { error: { code, message, retryable } } in both handlers.

findings.title: No Identity Propagation or Delegation Mechanism
findings.description: No mechanism to distinguish between an agent acting under its own service identity vs. acting on behalf of a specific human user. No token exchange, on-behalf-of flow, or dual-identity pattern exists.
findings.gap: Cannot distinguish agent identity modes (self vs delegated).
findings.recommendation: Design token model with acting_as claim to distinguish self-service vs delegated calls when implementing machine identity.

findings.title: No Immutable Audit Logging
findings.description: API Gateway access logging is enabled at INFO level. No CloudTrail configuration, no immutable log storage (S3 with object lock), and no log integrity validation defined in IaC.
findings.gap: No immutable, tamper-evident audit trail. CloudWatch logs are mutable.
findings.recommendation: Add AWS::CloudTrail::Trail with EnableLogFileValidation and S3 bucket with object lock for log storage.

findings.title: Cannot Suspend Agent Identity on Unauthenticated Endpoint
findings.description: GET /books requires no authentication. Without identity enforcement, there is no identity to suspend or revoke for read-only agent access. The only option is network-level blocking (WAF, IP rules) which is not configured.
findings.gap: Unauthenticated read endpoint means no agent identity lifecycle is possible.
findings.recommendation: Require authentication (API key or OAuth) on all agent-facing endpoints to enable identity-level suspension.

findings.title: No Compensation or Rollback for Write Operations
findings.description: CreateBook performs a simple DynamoDB putItem with no compensation logic, undo endpoint, or saga pattern. No mechanism to reverse operations if a workflow fails mid-sequence.
findings.gap: No compensation or rollback mechanism for write operations.
findings.recommendation: Add a DELETE /books/{isbn} endpoint and consider soft-delete with status field for reversibility.

findings.title: No Explicit Rate Limiting Configuration
findings.description: No explicit rate limiting or throttling configuration in IaC. No usage plans, per-method throttling, or WAF rate rules. Only default API Gateway account-level throttling (10,000 rps) applies.
findings.gap: No rate limiting to prevent runaway agent loops from overwhelming the service.
findings.recommendation: Add API Gateway usage plan with throttle settings and per-method throttling configuration.

findings.title: No Dedicated Sandbox for Agent Testing
findings.description: CI/CD pipeline deploys to staging environment. No dedicated sandbox for agent testing with production-equivalent data.
findings.gap: No dedicated agent testing sandbox environment.
findings.recommendation: Create dedicated sandbox stack with seeded test data for agent development.

findings.title: No Pagination or Result Size Limiting
findings.description: GET /books performs unbounded DynamoDB scan with no pagination, filters, or sorting. All books returned in single response.
findings.gap: Unbounded result sets exhaust agent context windows as dataset grows.
findings.recommendation: Add limit and exclusiveStartKey query parameters for DynamoDB pagination.

findings.title: No Input Validation or Schema Enforcement
findings.description: POST /books destructures request body without validation. No type checking, field presence validation, or format validation. Malformed input returns generic 500.
findings.gap: No input validation at API boundary. Malformed agent payloads fail silently.
findings.recommendation: Add request body validation (zod schema or API Gateway request validator) with structured error responses.

findings.title: No Temporal Metadata on Records
findings.description: DynamoDB items have no created_at, updated_at, or event_time fields. API responses include no freshness indicators.
findings.gap: Agents cannot determine when data was last updated or whether it is stale.
findings.recommendation: Add created_at and updated_at attributes. Include Last-Modified response headers.

findings.title: No Schema Versioning or Breaking Change Detection
findings.description: No API versioning (no /v1/ prefix, no Accept-Version header). No schema registry or breaking change detection in CI.
findings.gap: Agent tool bindings could break silently when the API changes.
findings.recommendation: Adopt URL-prefix versioning and add OpenAPI spec validation to CI pipeline.

findings.title: X-Ray Tracing Present but Structured Logging Absent
findings.description: X-Ray tracing enabled on Lambda and API Gateway. However, application logs use unstructured console.log without JSON formatting or correlation IDs.
findings.gap: Distributed tracing exists but structured logging with correlation IDs is absent.
findings.recommendation: Add @aws-lambda-powertools/logger for structured JSON logs with trace_id and correlation_id fields.

findings.title: Error Rate Alerting Present but Latency Alerting Absent
findings.description: CloudWatch alarms configured for Lambda function errors (threshold > 0). No latency-based alerting configured.
findings.gap: Latency degradation affecting agent responsiveness would go undetected.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch alarms for API Gateway Latency and IntegrationLatency at p95/p99 thresholds.

findings.title: No API Contract Testing in CI/CD
findings.description: Full CI/CD pipeline with unit and E2E tests. No API contract tests (Pact), OpenAPI validation, or breaking change detection tools.
findings.gap: Breaking API changes not caught automatically before affecting agents.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI spec validation or consumer-driven contract tests to the build stage.

findings.title: Test Coverage Lacks API Contract Validation
findings.description: Unit tests cover both handlers. E2E tests validate full API flow. No API contract tests or schema validation tests for agent consumption patterns.
findings.gap: No contract-level test coverage for agent-like consumption patterns.
findings.recommendation: Add API schema validation tests validating response structure and error formats.

findings.title: Credential Management — No Rotation Configured
findings.description: Credentials are stored in AWS SSM Parameter Store (SecureString) which is a valid secrets management service. Random password generation is used for Gitea admin credentials. However, no credential rotation is configured — SSM Parameter Store does not provide automatic rotation like AWS Secrets Manager.
findings.gap: No automatic credential rotation mechanism. SSM Parameter Store lacks native rotation support. No evidence of rotation schedules or Lambda-based rotation functions.
findings.recommendation: Migrate sensitive credentials (Gitea admin password, Flux tokens) from SSM Parameter Store to AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation enabled. Define rotation Lambda functions for non-IAM credentials.

findings.title: Immutable Audit Logging — Not Configured
findings.description: No CloudTrail configuration found in any Terraform module. No aws_cloudtrail resources defined. No immutable log storage (S3 with Object Lock). No CloudWatch Logs retention policies for audit purposes.
findings.gap: No audit trail infrastructure defined. Cannot attribute actions to specific principals in an immutable, tamper-evident log store.
findings.recommendation: Add aws_cloudtrail resource with S3 bucket (Object Lock enabled), CloudTrail log file validation, and multi-region trail. Enable EKS control plane logging.

findings.title: Agent Identity Suspension — No Immediate Mechanism
findings.description: IAM roles are defined in Terraform (IRSA roles for each service). While IAM inherently supports role deletion/policy detachment, there is no mechanism for immediate identity suspension without a full Terraform plan/apply cycle. No automated anomaly-triggered suspension.
findings.gap: No immediate suspension mechanism for agent identities. Revoking an IRSA role requires modifying Terraform state and re-applying, which is not an immediate response capability.
findings.recommendation: Implement automation (Lambda + EventBridge) that can immediately attach a deny-all inline policy to any IRSA role upon anomaly detection. Consider AWS IAM Access Analyzer for continuous monitoring.

findings.title: Distributed Tracing and Structured Logging — Not Configured
findings.description: No distributed tracing (X-Ray, OpenTelemetry) is configured in the repository. Microservices use basic Python logging module without structured JSON output or correlation IDs. No traceparent header propagation.
findings.gap: No distributed tracing infrastructure. No structured logging. No correlation ID propagation between services.
findings.recommendation: Deploy OpenTelemetry Collector as a Flux HelmRelease. Add ADOT DaemonSet. Update microservices to emit structured JSON logs with request_id correlation.

findings.title: Alerting on Error Rates and Latency — Not Configured
findings.description: No alerting infrastructure configured. No CloudWatch alarms, no Prometheus alerting rules, no PagerDuty/OpsGenie integration. Kubecost provides cost metrics only. README references Amazon Managed Grafana and Prometheus but these are not defined in the IaC.
findings.gap: No alerting thresholds for error rates, latency, or availability. No anomaly detection.
findings.recommendation: Deploy kube-prometheus-stack via Flux HelmRelease with pre-configured alerting rules for error rates (5xx), latency (p99), and pod restart counts. Integrate with SNS for alert routing.

findings.title: Infrastructure Governance — Partial Coverage
findings.description: Infrastructure is comprehensively defined as IaC (Terraform for AWS, Flux CD for Kubernetes). Drift detection is partially covered via Flux reconciliation and TF Controller. However, no branch protection, no PR review requirements, no AWS Config rules, and no terraform plan review gates in CI.
findings.gap: No peer review enforcement on IaC changes. No AWS Config drift detection. No plan-review gates in CI/CD.
findings.recommendation: Configure branch protection in Gitea requiring PR approval for main branch. Add Terraform plan step to CI. Deploy AWS Config conformance packs.

findings.title: CI/CD with API Contract Testing — No Tests
findings.description: CI/CD pipelines exist via Gitea Actions for all three microservices. Pipelines build Docker images and push to ECR with immutable tags. However, pipelines contain zero automated tests — no unit tests, no integration tests, no contract tests, no API validation.
findings.gap: No automated testing in CI. No API contract testing. No breaking change detection. No quality gates before deployment.
findings.recommendation: Add pytest stages to Gitea Actions workflows with API endpoint tests. Implement smoke tests post-deployment via Flux health checks. Consider Pact contract tests.

findings.title: API Test Coverage — Zero Coverage
findings.description: No automated test suites found. The only test artifact is a Helm chart test template (test-connection.yaml) that verifies HTTP connectivity. No pytest, no unittest, no integration test directories. CI pipelines skip testing entirely.
findings.gap: Zero automated test coverage for API endpoints or business logic.
findings.recommendation: Create test suites for each microservice covering endpoint response format, error handling, and SQS/DynamoDB integration. Add test execution to CI pipelines as a required gate.

findings.title: No Machine-Readable API Specification
findings.description: No OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, GraphQL schema, or machine-readable spec exists. 26+ REST endpoints defined only in PHP source code.
findings.gap: Agent tool generation requires manual authoring by reading source code.
findings.recommendation: Generate OpenAPI 3.0 specification documenting all endpoints, request/response schemas, and error codes.

findings.title: Unstructured Error Responses
findings.description: Error format is {"error": "message"} with HTTP status codes. No error codes, no retryable field, no field-level validation detail.
findings.gap: Agents cannot distinguish retriable from terminal errors programmatically.
findings.recommendation: Implement structured error codes with retryable indicators and field-level detail.

findings.title: No Identity Propagation or Delegation
findings.description: No identity propagation mechanism exists. No JWT parsing, no OAuth2 on-behalf-of flows, no token exchange patterns. All requests authenticated via PHP sessions with no distinction between agent-as-self vs agent-on-behalf-of-user.
findings.gap: Cannot distinguish an agent acting under its own identity vs acting on behalf of a specific user.
findings.recommendation: Implement JWT-based auth with token exchange support for delegated identity.

findings.title: No Secrets Management System
findings.description: Database credentials are passed directly as environment variables with hardcoded fallbacks. No AWS Secrets Manager, no HashiCorp Vault, no credential rotation mechanism.
findings.gap: Credentials are not managed through a secrets management system with rotation.
findings.recommendation: Migrate database credentials to AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation. Use RDS IAM authentication (already enabled on the RDS instance).

findings.title: No Immutable Audit Logging
findings.description: No immutable audit logging exists. order_status_history table records changes but is mutable database storage. No CloudTrail configuration in IaC. VPC Flow Logs capture network traffic only.
findings.gap: No tamper-evident audit trail for API actions. Cannot prove whether an action was taken by a human or an agent.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudTrail configuration. Implement structured application-level audit logging to CloudWatch Logs with immutable storage.

findings.title: No Agent Identity Suspension Mechanism
findings.description: No mechanism exists to suspend or revoke individual agent identities without affecting other users. Session-based auth only — no API keys to revoke, no service accounts to disable.
findings.gap: Cannot isolate a misbehaving agent without taking down the application or destroying all sessions.
findings.recommendation: Implement API key-based auth with per-key revocation. Add admin endpoint to disable individual service accounts.

findings.title: No Workflow-Level Compensation
findings.description: Database transactions with rollback exist for individual operations (order creation, return approval). However, the 7-step fulfillment workflow has no saga pattern or compensating actions if a downstream step fails.
findings.gap: Multi-step fulfillment workflow lacks compensation. Steps 1-4 cannot be rolled back if step 5 fails.
findings.recommendation: Implement saga pattern for fulfillment workflow with explicit compensating actions for each step.

findings.title: No Rate Limiting at API Layer
findings.description: No rate limiting at application level. WAF provides IP whitelisting only (no rate-based rules). App Runner MaxConcurrency (100) is a resource limit, not rate limiting.
findings.gap: No rate limits to prevent runaway agent loops from overwhelming the application.
findings.recommendation: Add WAF rate-based rules. Implement application-level rate limiting middleware or add API Gateway with usage plans.

findings.title: No Graceful Degradation Signaling
findings.description: No degraded mode signaling. No health headers, no X-Degraded, no Cache-Control, no Retry-After. Health checks detect only complete failure.
findings.gap: Agent cannot detect degraded responses vs authoritative data.
findings.recommendation: Add response headers for data freshness and implement granular health endpoint.

findings.title: Customer PII Exposed in Agent-Facing API Responses
findings.description: System stores customer PII (names, emails, addresses, payment records). Order endpoints return customer PII (email, name, shipping_address) without scoping. Password IS properly excluded.
findings.gap: Agent-facing APIs expose customer PII fields unnecessary for inventory/restocking decisions.
findings.recommendation: Create agent-specific endpoints or response DTOs that exclude customer PII. For restocking, agent only needs product_id, stock_quantity, warehouse_location.

findings.title: No Data Residency Controls
findings.description: System stores customer PII and payment data with no data residency documentation or policy. No controls preventing cross-region data transmission to LLM providers.
findings.gap: No data residency policy defined. No technical controls prevent customer data from being sent cross-region.
findings.recommendation: Document data residency requirements. Configure agent to use region-local Bedrock endpoints. Add data classification tags.

findings.title: No PII Redaction in Logs
findings.description: No PII redaction in logs. PHP error logging enabled without scrubbing. Customer data in exceptions could be logged without redaction.
findings.gap: Customer PII could appear in error logs. No automated PII detection or redaction.
findings.recommendation: Implement structured logging with PII field exclusion. Add CloudWatch Logs data protection policies.

findings.title: No Pagination or Filtering
findings.description: All query endpoints return complete result sets. No LIMIT, OFFSET, cursor, or filter parameters on list endpoints.
findings.gap: Unbounded result sets will exhaust LLM context windows.
findings.recommendation: Add pagination parameters (?limit=50&offset=0) and filter support to list endpoints.

findings.title: Minimal Input Validation
findings.description: Some required field checks and stock validation exist, but no systematic validation framework. No input length limits, no format validation, no type checking. Validation errors are unstructured.
findings.gap: Malformed agent payloads not rejected with field-level detail for self-correction.
findings.recommendation: Add validation layer with structured error responses including field name and constraint violated.

findings.title: No API-Level Freshness Signaling
findings.description: Database tables have created_at/updated_at fields. No Cache-Control headers, no freshness/staleness signaling in API responses.
findings.gap: Agent cannot determine whether data is current or stale from API responses alone.
findings.recommendation: Add Last-Modified or X-Data-Freshness headers. Store timestamps in explicit UTC.

findings.title: No Schema Versioning or API Contracts
findings.description: No API versioning, no schema files, no database migrations, no changelog, no breaking change detection, no contract tests.
findings.gap: API changes silently break agent tool bindings.
findings.recommendation: Add API version prefix (/v1/). Generate and commit OpenAPI spec. Add schema diff in CI.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing or Structured Logging
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry, no X-Ray, no structured logging. Only PHP error_log() producing unstructured text. No correlation IDs or request IDs.
findings.gap: Cannot reconstruct what happened during agent-initiated request failures.
findings.recommendation: Add AWS X-Ray SDK and structured JSON logging with request_id per request.

findings.title: No Alerting Configured
findings.description: No CloudWatch Alarms in CloudFormation. WAF metrics enabled but no alarms trigger. No PagerDuty/OpsGenie integration.
findings.gap: System degradation affecting agents not detected automatically.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch Alarms for HTTP 5xx rate, P99 latency, WAF blocked request spikes.

findings.title: IaC Without Review or Drift Detection
findings.description: CloudFormation IaC exists but no CI/CD enforces review before deployment. No drift detection. Manual deployment via deploy.sh.
findings.gap: Infrastructure changes not subject to peer review or drift monitoring.
findings.recommendation: Add CI/CD pipeline with required PR approval. Enable CloudFormation drift detection.

findings.title: No CI/CD Pipeline
findings.description: No CI/CD pipeline. No GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkinsfile, or buildspec.yml. Deployment is manual via deploy.sh.
findings.gap: API contract changes cannot be caught before agents are affected.
findings.recommendation: Create CI/CD pipeline with API contract testing and schema validation.

findings.title: No Automated Rollback
findings.description: No automated rollback capability. ECR immutable tags allow manual previous-image deployment. No automatic rollback triggers.
findings.gap: Broken deployment requires manual intervention to roll back.
findings.recommendation: Configure App Runner auto-rollback on health check failure or add CodeDeploy with rollback triggers.

findings.title: Zero Test Coverage
findings.description: No test files exist. No PHPUnit, no API test suites, no integration tests, no contract tests. Zero test infrastructure.
findings.gap: No verification that API behavior matches expectations. Silent breakage possible.
findings.recommendation: Add PHPUnit integration tests for agent-facing endpoints (GET /api/products, GET /api/orders/{id}/validation-data).

findings.title: No Machine-Readable API Specification
findings.description: No OpenAPI, Swagger, AsyncAPI, or machine-readable spec exists. API defined only in Java annotations.
findings.gap: Agent tool definitions must be manually authored. Drift between tools and implementation likely.
findings.recommendation: Add springdoc-openapi-ui dependency to auto-generate OpenAPI spec.

findings.title: No Structured Error Responses
findings.description: Error responses are bare HTTP status codes with empty bodies. ResponseEntity(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST) with no error detail.
findings.gap: Agent cannot distinguish retriable from terminal errors or identify failed fields.
findings.recommendation: Implement @ControllerAdvice with structured JSON error bodies.

findings.title: No Scoped Permissions
findings.description: All endpoints use @PreAuthorize("permitAll()"). No role differentiation, no scoped permissions, no mechanism to grant read-only vs read-write access per caller.
findings.gap: Cannot create a scoped agent identity with restricted access.
findings.recommendation: Implement RBAC with read-only and read-write roles enforced at Spring Security or API Gateway level.

findings.title: No Action-Level Authorization
findings.description: No action-level authorization. All endpoints (read and write) open to any caller with identical access.
findings.gap: Cannot restrict an agent to read-only access while allowing another agent write access.
findings.recommendation: Implement method-level security annotations or API Gateway method-level auth.

findings.title: No Identity Propagation
findings.description: No identity propagation. User context passed as URL parameter (userUuid), not from authenticated token. Cannot distinguish agent-as-self vs agent-on-behalf-of-user.
findings.gap: Cannot determine whether an API call was made by an agent under its own authority or on behalf of a specific user.
findings.recommendation: Implement JWT-based identity propagation with both agent and delegated user identity.

findings.title: Hardcoded Database Credentials
findings.description: Database credentials hardcoded in application.properties: username MonoToMicroUser and password MonoToMicroPassword. No secrets management system used.
findings.gap: Hardcoded credentials with no rotation. Prompt injection or config exposure leaks database access.
findings.recommendation: Migrate credentials to AWS Secrets Manager with IAM-based access and automatic rotation.

findings.title: No Audit Logging
findings.description: No audit logging exists. No CloudTrail, no application-level audit log, no immutable log storage. Only System.out.println for EC2 metadata in HealthController.
findings.gap: Cannot determine who made any API call, when, or what the outcome was.
findings.recommendation: Implement structured audit logging with principal attribution stored in CloudWatch Logs or S3 with Object Lock.

findings.title: No Agent Identity Suspension Mechanism
findings.description: No identity management system exists. Authentication is disabled (permitAll()), so no mechanism to revoke or suspend any identity.
findings.gap: Cannot isolate or disable a misbehaving agent without taking down the entire application.
findings.recommendation: Implement API key or OAuth2 client identity with revocation mechanism.

findings.title: No Compensation or Rollback
findings.description: No compensation or rollback mechanisms. Basket operations are single INSERT/DELETE statements with no transaction coordination. No saga pattern, no undo endpoints.
findings.gap: Partial state cannot be reverted if multi-step workflows fail.
findings.recommendation: Implement compensation endpoints and wrap multi-step operations in database transactions.

findings.title: No Rate Limiting
findings.description: No rate limiting at any layer. No API Gateway, no WAF, no application-level middleware. Application accepts unlimited requests.
findings.gap: Runaway agent loops can overwhelm the application at machine speed.
findings.recommendation: Deploy API Gateway with throttling or add rate limiting middleware.

findings.title: No Graceful Degradation Signaling
findings.description: Health endpoints provide binary status only. No machine-readable degradation indicators or freshness headers.
findings.gap: Agents cannot detect degraded mode operation.
findings.recommendation: Enhance health endpoints with granular JSON status. Add X-Data-Freshness headers.

findings.title: No Sandbox/Staging Environment
findings.description: No sandbox, staging, or local development environment configuration. No Docker Compose, no environment profiles.
findings.gap: No safe environment to test agent behavior.
findings.recommendation: Create Docker Compose setup with MySQL for local/staging agent testing.

findings.title: Sensitive Data Exposed via API Without Filtering
findings.description: User PII (email, first_name, last_name) stored in unicorn_user table and returned via API responses with no filtering. Login endpoint returns full User object including email.
findings.gap: B1: PII fields (email) exposed in API responses. B2: No access control differentiation. B3: No formal classification.
findings.recommendation: Create agent-facing response DTOs excluding PII. Implement OAuth scopes for data access differentiation.

findings.title: No Data Residency Controls
findings.description: No data residency configuration. User PII stored with no documented residency requirements or region constraints.
findings.gap: No mechanism to prevent agent from transmitting PII to LLM endpoint in different region.
findings.recommendation: Document data residency requirements. Configure agent orchestration for same-region LLM endpoints.

findings.title: No PII Redaction Controls in Logs
findings.description: No PII redaction controls exist. Current logging is minimal but no safeguards against PII leakage if log verbosity increases.
findings.gap: No systematic PII protection in logging pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Implement structured logging with PII field masking before enabling detailed logging.

findings.title: No Pagination or Filtering
findings.description: GET /unicorns returns ALL records. Unbounded SELECT queries with no LIMIT, pagination, or filtering.
findings.gap: Agents receive entire datasets. Context window exhaustion risk.
findings.recommendation: Add pagination parameters to list endpoints.

findings.title: Minimal Input Validation
findings.description: Only null checks in controllers. No validation framework, no @Valid annotations, no structured validation errors. SQL uses parameterized queries preventing injection.
findings.gap: Malformed agent payloads not rejected with useful feedback.
findings.recommendation: Add spring-boot-starter-validation with Bean Validation annotations.

findings.title: Temporal Metadata Not Exposed
findings.description: Database has creation_date and last_modified_date columns but API does not expose them. No freshness headers.
findings.gap: Agents cannot determine data freshness from API responses.
findings.recommendation: Map timestamp fields to response DTOs. Add Last-Modified headers.

findings.title: No Schema Versioning or API Contracts
findings.description: No API versioning, no schema versioning, no breaking change detection, no CI/CD pipeline.
findings.gap: Agent tool bindings break silently on API changes.
findings.recommendation: Version API paths and implement contract testing in CI.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing or Structured Logging
findings.description: No X-Ray, no OpenTelemetry, no structured logging. Only System.out.println. No correlation IDs.
findings.gap: Cannot trace or debug agent-initiated requests.
findings.recommendation: Add X-Ray SDK or OpenTelemetry. Implement structured JSON logging.

findings.title: No Alerting Configuration
findings.description: No CloudWatch alarms, no anomaly detection, no PagerDuty/OpsGenie integration.
findings.gap: Cannot detect target system degradation proactively.
findings.recommendation: Deploy CloudWatch alarms for error rates and latency.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No IaC exists. No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, or Helm. Infrastructure presumably managed manually.
findings.gap: Agent-facing infrastructure is unauditable and non-reproducible.
findings.recommendation: Define infrastructure in CloudFormation or CDK.

findings.title: No CI/CD Pipeline
findings.description: No CI/CD configuration. No GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, or CodeBuild.
findings.gap: API changes deployed without automated verification.
findings.recommendation: Implement CI/CD with contract testing.

findings.title: No Rollback Capability
findings.description: No deployment or rollback configuration. Manual JAR deployment to EC2.
findings.gap: Cannot roll back broken deployments affecting agent APIs.
findings.recommendation: Implement CodeDeploy with health-check-based rollback.

findings.title: Zero Test Coverage
findings.description: No test files exist. Test dependency in build.gradle unused. No unit, integration, or API tests.
findings.gap: No automated verification of API behavior.
findings.recommendation: Create integration tests for controller endpoints.

findings.title: Encryption at Rest Unverifiable
findings.description: No IaC to verify KMS encryption on database storage. No KMS references.
findings.gap: PII may be unencrypted at rest.
findings.recommendation: Enable RDS/EBS encryption with KMS. Document in IaC.

findings.title: Write Operations Lack Idempotency Keys
findings.description: POST endpoints lack idempotency keys. Product creation uses server-generated UUID. Checkout has no duplicate protection.
findings.gap: Non-idempotent writes risk duplication on retry.
findings.recommendation: Add idempotency key support before enabling write-enabled agent scope.

findings.title: Structured JSON Response Format
findings.description: All responses are structured JSON with consistent envelope { message, body }. Well-suited for LLM consumption.
findings.gap: None — format is appropriate.
findings.recommendation: Document response schemas in OpenAPI spec.

findings.title: Partial Event Coverage for State Changes
findings.description: Basket checkout emits EventBridge events. Product CRUD and order creation do not emit events.
findings.gap: Only checkout observable via events. Product/order changes invisible to reactive agents.
findings.recommendation: Add events for product/order state changes if event-reactive agents planned.

findings.title: No Rate Limit Documentation or Headers
findings.description: No rate limit documentation. No rate limit response headers. API Gateway defaults apply silently.
findings.gap: Agents cannot self-throttle based on quota signals.
findings.recommendation: Configure usage plans and return X-RateLimit-Remaining headers.

findings.title: No Immutable Audit Logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail, no immutable audit storage, no principal attribution in logs.
findings.gap: No audit trail for API calls.
findings.recommendation: Enable CloudTrail with log validation when auth is implemented.

findings.title: Multi-Step Operations Without Compensation
findings.description: Checkout flow has sequential steps (get basket → publish event → delete basket) without compensation logic for partial failures.
findings.gap: Partial failures leave inconsistent state.
findings.recommendation: Implement compensation logic before expanding to write-enabled agents.

findings.title: No Concurrency Controls
findings.description: No optimistic locking, version fields, or conditional writes in DynamoDB operations.
findings.gap: No concurrency protection for writes.
findings.recommendation: Add conditional writes before enabling write-enabled agents.

findings.title: No Transaction or Blast Radius Limits
findings.description: No per-caller operation caps. Scan operations return unbounded results. No bulk operation limits.
findings.gap: No business-domain caps on operation scope.
findings.recommendation: Add pagination limits and per-caller caps.

findings.title: No Draft/Pending State Mechanism
findings.description: All writes immediately committed. No reviewable intermediate state.
findings.gap: No mechanism for human review of agent-proposed changes.
findings.recommendation: Add status fields for future write-enabled agents.

findings.title: No Configurable Approval Gates
findings.description: No approval workflow or human-in-the-loop gates.
findings.gap: No mechanism for human oversight of agent operations.
findings.recommendation: Consider Step Functions human approval tasks.

findings.title: No Sandbox/Staging Environment
findings.description: No staging environment. Single CDK stack. No local testing setup.
findings.gap: Agent testing only possible in production.
findings.recommendation: Create separate staging stack with synthetic data.

findings.title: No Data Quality Awareness
findings.description: No data quality monitoring, completeness metrics, or data lifecycle management.
findings.gap: Agent may reason on stale data without awareness.
findings.recommendation: Implement DynamoDB TTL on basket table.

findings.title: No Data Catalog or Metadata Layer
findings.description: No data catalog. DynamoDB schemas documented only in CDK code comments.
findings.gap: Agent tool builders must read source for data semantics.
findings.recommendation: Create schema reference documentation.

findings.title: No Business Outcome Metrics
findings.description: No custom business metrics published. No PutMetricData calls.
findings.gap: Cannot measure agent interaction quality.
findings.recommendation: Publish order completion and checkout success metrics.

findings.title: Business Outcome Metrics — Cost Only
findings.description: Kubecost provides cost-per-tenant metrics. Metrics Server provides resource utilization for HPA. No custom business outcome metrics are published (e.g., tenant onboarding success rate, message processing throughput, payment completion rate).
findings.gap: No business-level metrics beyond cost. No tenant lifecycle success/failure tracking.
findings.recommendation: Publish custom CloudWatch metrics for tenant lifecycle operations (onboarding duration, deployment success/failure rate) and per-tenant message throughput from the microservices.

findings.title: Write Endpoints Not Idempotent
findings.description: No idempotency keys on POST endpoints. uniqid() used for ID generation. Repeated POST creates duplicate records.
findings.gap: Duplicate creation risk on retry when write scope is enabled.
findings.recommendation: Implement idempotency keys when write-enabled scope is planned.

findings.title: JSON Response Format
findings.description: All API responses use structured JSON format with Content-Type: application/json. Well-structured with meaningful key names.
findings.gap: None — format is ideal for agent consumption.
findings.recommendation: No action needed.

findings.title: No Event Emission for State Changes
findings.description: No event emission capability. No webhooks, no SNS/EventBridge, no Kafka. State changes stored only in database.
findings.gap: Agents cannot react proactively to state changes. Polling is the only detection mechanism.
findings.recommendation: Add EventBridge events when stock_quantity drops below restock threshold.

findings.title: No Rate Limit Headers
findings.description: No X-RateLimit-Remaining or Retry-After headers in API responses. System capacity limits not communicated to callers.
findings.gap: Agent has no visibility into system capacity. Cannot self-throttle.
findings.recommendation: Add rate limit headers once rate limiting is implemented.

findings.title: No Concurrency Controls
findings.description: No optimistic locking, no version fields, no ETags. Atomic SQL decrements provide basic concurrency safety for inventory.
findings.gap: No broad concurrency control pattern for write operations.
findings.recommendation: Add version fields when write scope is planned.

findings.title: No Transaction Limits
findings.description: No per-caller limits on records modified, no spend caps, no operation count limits.
findings.gap: No business-domain caps on agent action volume.
findings.recommendation: Add per-agent transaction limits when write scope is planned.

findings.title: Pending/Draft State Exists for Returns
findings.description: Returns system has pending_review status. Orders start as pending. These are existing reviewable intermediate states.
findings.gap: Pattern exists for returns only, not extensible to other operations.
findings.recommendation: No action for read-only scope.

findings.title: Hard-Coded Approval Gate for Returns
findings.description: Return approval requires explicit admin action. Not configurable per operation type.
findings.gap: Approval gates are not configurable or extensible.
findings.recommendation: No action for read-only scope.

findings.title: No Data Quality Metrics
findings.description: No data quality scores, completeness metrics, null rate monitoring, or freshness SLAs.
findings.gap: Agent cannot assess data reliability.
findings.recommendation: Add basic quality checks for inventory data freshness and accuracy.

findings.title: Semantically Meaningful Field Names
findings.description: All field names are human-readable: customer_name, stock_quantity, shipping_address, total_amount. No legacy abbreviations.
findings.gap: None — naming is clear and agent-friendly.
findings.recommendation: No action needed.

findings.title: No Data Catalog or Metadata Layer
findings.description: No data catalog. Schema defined only in PHP CREATE TABLE statements.
findings.gap: Schema understanding requires reading source code.
findings.recommendation: Generate data dictionary documentation.

findings.title: No Business Outcome Metrics
findings.description: No custom CloudWatch metrics for business outcomes. No order fulfillment rate or inventory turnover tracking.
findings.gap: Cannot measure agent effectiveness on business outcomes.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch custom metrics for fulfillment rate, stockout events, average fulfillment time.

findings.title: Partial Idempotency via Database Constraints
findings.description: INSERT IGNORE and UNIQUE constraints provide database-level idempotency for basket operations. No explicit idempotency key support.
findings.gap: No request-level idempotency mechanism for general write operations.
findings.recommendation: Implement idempotency keys when expanding to write-enabled scope.

findings.title: JSON Response Format
findings.description: All API responses use JSON via Jackson. Agent-friendly format requiring no additional parsing.
findings.gap: None.
findings.recommendation: No action needed.

findings.title: No Event Emission
findings.description: No event emission. Internal event objects are in-process DTOs, not distributed events. No webhooks, SNS, SQS, or EventBridge.
findings.gap: Agents cannot subscribe to state changes reactively.
findings.recommendation: Add EventBridge integration during decomposition.

findings.title: No Rate Limit Headers
findings.description: No rate limit documentation or X-RateLimit-Remaining/Retry-After headers.
findings.gap: Agents have no visibility into call budget.
findings.recommendation: Add rate limit headers when implementing STATE-Q5 rate limiting.

findings.title: No Concurrency Controls
findings.description: No optimistic locking, no version fields, no ETags. INSERT IGNORE prevents duplicates but not general concurrency control.
findings.gap: Concurrent writes could cause race conditions under write-enabled scope.
findings.recommendation: Add version fields and optimistic locking when expanding scope.

findings.title: No Transaction Limits
findings.description: No transaction limits or blast radius controls. No configurable limits on operations per session.
findings.gap: Under write-enabled scope, unbounded operations possible.
findings.recommendation: Implement per-caller transaction limits for write-enabled scope.

findings.title: No Draft/Pending State
findings.description: No draft or pending state. All writes commit immediately.
findings.gap: No human review opportunity for agent-proposed changes.
findings.recommendation: Add PENDING status for write operations under write-enabled scope.

findings.title: No Approval Gates
findings.description: No approval gate mechanism. All operations execute immediately.
findings.gap: Cannot configure human approval for specific operations.
findings.recommendation: Consider Step Functions approval tasks for high-risk operations.

findings.title: No Data Quality Metrics
findings.description: No data quality monitoring, profiling, or completeness metrics.
findings.gap: Cannot assess data reliability for agent decisions.
findings.recommendation: Add data quality monitoring.

findings.title: No Data Catalog
findings.description: No data catalog or metadata layer. Only schema reference is create_tables.sql.
findings.gap: Agent tool builders must reverse-engineer the data model.
findings.recommendation: Document data model in structured format.

findings.title: No Business Outcome Metrics
findings.description: No custom business metrics. Only infrastructure metrics via Spring Boot Actuator.
findings.gap: Cannot measure agent interaction quality.
findings.recommendation: Publish business metrics (basket operations, registrations) to CloudWatch.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Machine Identity Authentication
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: No machine identity authentication exists across 4 services. All affected services lack OAuth2 client credentials, API keys with principal attribution, or mTLS for machine-to-machine authentication.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Services were designed for human browser-based access only. No M2M authentication pattern was implemented during original development.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Deploy a shared Cognito User Pool with resource server and client_credentials grant, or implement API Gateway with IAM authorization across all services.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: Every agent API call authenticated with machine-identifiable credential logged with principal attribution.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Identity Propagation and Delegation
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: No identity propagation mechanism exists. No JWT parsing, no OAuth2 on-behalf-of flows, no token exchange patterns. All requests authenticated via PHP sessions with no distinction between agent-as-self vs agent-on-behalf-of-user.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Cannot distinguish an agent acting under its own identity vs acting on behalf of a specific user.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Implement JWT-based auth with token exchange support for delegated identity.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 4 affected services address Identity Propagation and Delegation.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Credential Management
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: Database credentials are passed directly as environment variables with hardcoded fallbacks. No AWS Secrets Manager, no HashiCorp Vault, no credential rotation mechanism.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Credentials are not managed through a secrets management system with rotation.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Migrate database credentials to AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation. Use RDS IAM authentication (already enabled on the RDS instance).
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 3 affected services address Credential Management.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Immutable Audit Logging
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: No immutable audit logging exists. order_status_history table records changes but is mutable database storage. No CloudTrail configuration in IaC. VPC Flow Logs capture network traffic only.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: No tamper-evident audit trail for API actions. Cannot prove whether an action was taken by a human or an agent.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Add CloudTrail configuration. Implement structured application-level audit logging to CloudWatch Logs with immutable storage.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 4 affected services address Immutable Audit Logging.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Agent Identity Suspension
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: No mechanism exists to suspend or revoke individual agent identities without affecting other users. Session-based auth only — no API keys to revoke, no service accounts to disable.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Cannot isolate a misbehaving agent without taking down the application or destroying all sessions.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Implement API key-based auth with per-key revocation. Add admin endpoint to disable individual service accounts.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 5 affected services address Agent Identity Suspension.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Sensitive Data Classification
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: System stores customer PII (names, emails, addresses, payment records). Order endpoints return customer PII (email, name, shipping_address) without scoping. Password IS properly excluded.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Agent-facing APIs expose customer PII fields unnecessary for inventory/restocking decisions.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Create agent-specific endpoints or response DTOs that exclude customer PII. For restocking, agent only needs product_id, stock_quantity, warehouse_location.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 3 affected services address Sensitive Data Classification.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Data Residency and Sovereignty
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: System stores customer PII and payment data with no data residency documentation or policy. No controls preventing cross-region data transmission to LLM providers.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: No data residency policy defined. No technical controls prevent customer data from being sent cross-region.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Document data residency requirements. Configure agent to use region-local Bedrock endpoints. Add data classification tags.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 3 affected services address Data Residency and Sovereignty.

cross_cutting_findings.title: PII Redaction in Logs
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: No PII redaction in logs. PHP error logging enabled without scrubbing. Customer data in exceptions could be logged without redaction.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Customer PII could appear in error logs. No automated PII detection or redaction.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Implement structured logging with PII field exclusion. Add CloudWatch Logs data protection policies.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 3 affected services address PII Redaction in Logs.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Compensation and Rollback
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: Database transactions with rollback exist for individual operations (order creation, return approval). However, the 7-step fulfillment workflow has no saga pattern or compensating actions if a downstream step fails.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Multi-step fulfillment workflow lacks compensation. Steps 1-4 cannot be rolled back if step 5 fails.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Implement saga pattern for fulfillment workflow with explicit compensating actions for each step.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 3 affected services address Compensation and Rollback.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Rate Limiting and Throttling
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: No rate limiting at application level. WAF provides IP whitelisting only (no rate-based rules). App Runner MaxConcurrency (100) is a resource limit, not rate limiting.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: No rate limits to prevent runaway agent loops from overwhelming the application.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Add WAF rate-based rules. Implement application-level rate limiting middleware or add API Gateway with usage plans.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 4 affected services address Rate Limiting and Throttling.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Machine-Readable API Specification
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: No OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, GraphQL schema, or machine-readable spec exists. 26+ REST endpoints defined only in PHP source code.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Agent tool generation requires manual authoring by reading source code.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Generate OpenAPI 3.0 specification documenting all endpoints, request/response schemas, and error codes.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 4 affected services address Machine-Readable API Specification.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Structured Error Responses
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: Error format is {"error": "message"} with HTTP status codes. No error codes, no retryable field, no field-level validation detail.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Agents cannot distinguish retriable from terminal errors programmatically.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Implement structured error codes with retryable indicators and field-level detail.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 4 affected services address Structured Error Responses.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Selective Query Support
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: All query endpoints return complete result sets. No LIMIT, OFFSET, cursor, or filter parameters on list endpoints.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Unbounded result sets will exhaust LLM context windows.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Add pagination parameters (?limit=50&offset=0) and filter support to list endpoints.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 4 affected services address Selective Query Support.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Input Validation and Schema Enforcement
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: Some required field checks and stock validation exist, but no systematic validation framework. No input length limits, no format validation, no type checking. Validation errors are unstructured.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Malformed agent payloads not rejected with field-level detail for self-correction.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Add validation layer with structured error responses including field name and constraint violated.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 4 affected services address Input Validation and Schema Enforcement.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Temporal Metadata and Freshness
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: Database tables have created_at/updated_at fields. No Cache-Control headers, no freshness/staleness signaling in API responses.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Agent cannot determine whether data is current or stale from API responses alone.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Add Last-Modified or X-Data-Freshness headers. Store timestamps in explicit UTC.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 4 affected services address Temporal Metadata and Freshness.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Schema Versioning and API Contracts
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: No API versioning, no schema files, no database migrations, no changelog, no breaking change detection, no contract tests.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: API changes silently break agent tool bindings.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Add API version prefix (/v1/). Generate and commit OpenAPI spec. Add schema diff in CI.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 4 affected services address Schema Versioning and API Contracts.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Infrastructure Governance
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: CloudFormation IaC exists but no CI/CD enforces review before deployment. No drift detection. Manual deployment via deploy.sh.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Infrastructure changes not subject to peer review or drift monitoring.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Add CI/CD pipeline with required PR approval. Enable CloudFormation drift detection.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 4 affected services address Infrastructure Governance.

cross_cutting_findings.title: CI/CD with API Contract Testing
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: No CI/CD pipeline. No GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkinsfile, or buildspec.yml. Deployment is manual via deploy.sh.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: API contract changes cannot be caught before agents are affected.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Create CI/CD pipeline with API contract testing and schema validation.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 5 affected services address CI/CD with API Contract Testing.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Rollback Capability
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: No automated rollback capability. ECR immutable tags allow manual previous-image deployment. No automatic rollback triggers.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Broken deployment requires manual intervention to roll back.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Configure App Runner auto-rollback on health check failure or add CodeDeploy with rollback triggers.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 3 affected services address Rollback Capability.

cross_cutting_findings.title: API Test Coverage
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: No test files exist. No PHPUnit, no API test suites, no integration tests, no contract tests. Zero test infrastructure.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: No verification that API behavior matches expectations. Silent breakage possible.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Add PHPUnit integration tests for agent-facing endpoints (GET /api/products, GET /api/orders/{id}/validation-data).
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 5 affected services address API Test Coverage.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Distributed Tracing and Structured Logging
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: No OpenTelemetry, no X-Ray, no structured logging. Only PHP error_log() producing unstructured text. No correlation IDs or request IDs.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Cannot reconstruct what happened during agent-initiated request failures.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Add AWS X-Ray SDK and structured JSON logging with request_id per request.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 5 affected services address Distributed Tracing and Structured Logging.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Alerting on Error Rates and Latency
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: No CloudWatch Alarms in CloudFormation. WAF metrics enabled but no alarms trigger. No PagerDuty/OpsGenie integration.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: System degradation affecting agents not detected automatically.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Add CloudWatch Alarms for HTTP 5xx rate, P99 latency, WAF blocked request spikes.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 5 affected services address Alerting on Error Rates and Latency.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Graceful Degradation Signaling
cross_cutting_findings.common_finding_summary: No degraded mode signaling. No health headers, no X-Degraded, no Cache-Control, no Retry-After. Health checks detect only complete failure.
cross_cutting_findings.root_cause_pattern: Agent cannot detect degraded responses vs authoritative data.

portfolio_remediation.immediate_action: Add response headers for data freshness and implement granular health endpoint.
portfolio_remediation.target_state: All 3 affected services address Graceful Degradation Signaling.

items.common_finding_summary: No machine identity authentication across affected services.
items.root_cause_pattern: Services designed for human access only, no M2M auth.
items.remediation: Implement API key authentication with principal attribution alongside existing session auth. Add Authorization: Bearer <api-key> header check mapping keys to named service accounts.

remediation_detail.immediate_action: Deploy shared auth (Cognito/API Gateway IAM)
remediation_detail.target_state: All agent calls authenticated with principal attribution

items.common_finding_summary: No identity propagation mechanism exists. No JWT parsing, no OAuth2 on-behalf-of flows, no token exchange patterns. All requests authenticated via PHP sessions with no distinction between agent-as-self vs agent-on-behalf-of-user.
items.root_cause_pattern: Cannot distinguish an agent acting under its own identity vs acting on behalf of a specific user.
items.remediation: Implement JWT-based auth with token exchange support for delegated identity.

remediation_detail.immediate_action: Implement JWT-based auth with token exchange support for delegated identity.
remediation_detail.target_state: All services address Identity Propagation and Delegation

items.common_finding_summary: Database credentials are passed directly as environment variables with hardcoded fallbacks. No AWS Secrets Manager, no HashiCorp Vault, no credential rotation mechanism.
items.root_cause_pattern: Credentials are not managed through a secrets management system with rotation.
items.remediation: Migrate database credentials to AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation. Use RDS IAM authentication (already enabled on the RDS instance).

remediation_detail.immediate_action: Migrate database credentials to AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation. Use RDS IAM authentication (already enabled on the RDS instance).
remediation_detail.target_state: All services address Credential Management

items.root_cause_pattern: No tamper-evident audit trail for API actions. Cannot prove whether an action was taken by a human or an agent.
items.remediation: Add CloudTrail configuration. Implement structured application-level audit logging to CloudWatch Logs with immutable storage.

remediation_detail.immediate_action: Add CloudTrail configuration. Implement structured application-level audit logging to CloudWatch Logs with immutable storage.
remediation_detail.target_state: All services address Immutable Audit Logging

items.common_finding_summary: No mechanism exists to suspend or revoke individual agent identities without affecting other users. Session-based auth only — no API keys to revoke, no service accounts to disable.
items.root_cause_pattern: Cannot isolate a misbehaving agent without taking down the application or destroying all sessions.
items.remediation: Implement API key-based auth with per-key revocation. Add admin endpoint to disable individual service accounts.

items.remediation: Create agent-specific endpoints or response DTOs that exclude customer PII. For restocking, agent only needs product_id, stock_quantity, warehouse_location.

portfolio_level_findings.title: Centralized Identity Plane
portfolio_level_findings.finding: No shared identity provider detected across the portfolio. books-api uses Cognito for human users but no client_credentials flow for M2M. eks-saas-gitops uses IRSA for in-cluster workloads but this is not shared by other services. No common IdP or shared auth mechanism spans multiple repositories.
portfolio_level_findings.evidence: books-api: template.yml (Cognito UserPool for human auth only); eks-saas-gitops: terraform/modules/gitops-saas-infra/main.tf (IRSA roles); other services: no auth configured
portfolio_level_findings.recommendation: Deploy a shared Cognito User Pool with a resource server and custom scopes (or a shared API Gateway with IAM authorization) that all services reference for agent M2M authentication.

portfolio_level_findings.title: Cross-Service Audit Correlation
portfolio_level_findings.finding: No shared trace ID propagation or centralized audit trail detected across the portfolio. books-api has X-Ray tracing enabled. No other service has distributed tracing. No shared CloudTrail trail configuration. No consistent correlation ID headers across services.
portfolio_level_findings.evidence: books-api: template.yml (X-Ray tracing on Lambda/API Gateway); all others: no tracing configured
portfolio_level_findings.recommendation: Deploy a shared CloudTrail trail covering all accounts/services. Standardize on X-Amzn-Trace-Id header propagation. Deploy OpenTelemetry Collector on EKS for services migrating there.

portfolio_level_findings.title: Portfolio-Level Rate Limiting
portfolio_level_findings.finding: No shared WAF or API gateway protecting the portfolio perimeter. local-monolith has its own WAF configuration. Individual services have varying levels of rate limiting (books-api relies on API Gateway defaults, aws-microservices has no explicit rate limiting). Rate limiting exists only at individual service level.
portfolio_level_findings.evidence: local-monolith: infrastructure/monolith-apprunner.yaml (WAF WebACL); books-api: API Gateway default throttling; others: no rate limiting
portfolio_level_findings.recommendation: For agent-at-scale scenarios, implement a shared WAF WebACL or API Gateway with usage plans that protects all agent-facing endpoints with consistent rate limits.

portfolio_level_findings.title: Transitive Dependency Safety
portfolio_level_findings.finding: No explicit dependency overrides provided. Based on portfolio context, services running on the EKS platform (eks-saas-gitops) depend on its infrastructure readiness. Since eks-saas-gitops is Pilot-Ready (Safety Concerns) and not Agent-Ready, services deployed on it inherit its safety constraints. However, no service in the portfolio is currently Agent-Ready or Pilot-Ready without safety concerns, so transitive blocking is not immediately actionable.
portfolio_level_findings.evidence: eks-saas-gitops: Pilot-Ready (Safety Concerns); all application services: Remediation Required
portfolio_level_findings.recommendation: Add dependency_overrides to portfolio config to enable precise transitive analysis. Prioritize resolving eks-saas-gitops safety concerns before deploying agent-enabled workloads on the cluster.

portfolio_level_findings.title: Agent Identity Governance
portfolio_level_findings.finding: No centralized mechanism to suspend or revoke agent identities across all services simultaneously. Each service manages identity independently (or has no identity management at all). No shared Cognito app client registry, no centralized API key management, no portfolio-level agent identity kill switch.
portfolio_level_findings.evidence: books-api: Cognito (human auth only, no M2M); eks-saas-gitops: IRSA (cluster-scoped only); others: no auth mechanism
portfolio_level_findings.recommendation: Implement a centralized agent identity registry (Cognito app clients with resource servers, or a shared API key management system) with an emergency revocation mechanism (e.g., Lambda function that disables all agent app clients or rotates all API keys).
